text: As you can see, the missing pericardium, the head is to the top right and lateral is to the bottom right.
The second, third, and fourth ribs are labeled and the lung where it was stapled is also seen.
This is another view of the intraoperative resection view turned around.

the lung to the right lateral posterior right bottom head to the top right and second third and fourth ribs which can easily be seen the pericardium is again missing here we made sure to avoid the phrenic nerve this is the intraoperative reconstruction with the jp drain in place

Here you can clearly see the JP drain is exiting with the head to the left, the feet to the right, the left side of the patient at the top of the screen, and the dual mesh with the rough portion externally facing and number one proline sutures used interrupted to sew the mesh into place.
